hypothesis: If a diffusion model framework combining an equivariant graph neural network backbone with an energy-based denoising approach that incorporates Hamiltonian Monte Carlo refinement is applied to amorphous material generation, then it will produce structurally valid amorphous configurations with radial distribution functions, bond angle distributions, and structure factors that closely match reference molecular dynamics simulations, because enforcing physical energy constraints during the reverse diffusion process guides generated structures toward realistic local geometries that standard score-matching alone cannot guarantee.
